About
01

Engineering
systems that
think.

I'm a final-year BEng (Hons) Electrical and Electronic Engineering student at Aston University, driven by a passion for control systems, embedded firmware, and power electronics.

My dissertation focused on designing and implementing a Traction Control System for an RC vehicle using dual-core FreeRTOS on an ESP32 — a project spanning hardware design, PCB fabrication, MATLAB/Simulink modelling, and real-time embedded C++.

I'm a student member of the Institution of Engineering and Technology (IET) and an active member of Aston Electronics Society. I approach engineering problems analytically: model first, validate in simulation, then build and iterate with measured data.

Projects
03
Dissertation Embedded FreeRTOS
01

Traction Control System for an RC Vehicle

Final Year Dissertation · Aston University · 2024–25

Designed, built, and validated a full traction control system from the ground up — spanning custom PCB hardware, dual-core ESP32 firmware, and a MATLAB/Simulink control model. The system uses Hall-effect wheel speed sensors and an IMU to detect slip in real time, applying a discrete PID controller to regulate ESC output.

  • 50–63% wheel slip reduction at low/mid throttle on wood surface — exceeding 25% SMART objective
  • Dual-core FreeRTOS architecture: Core 0 handles sensor acquisition, Core 1 runs PID control loop
  • Exponential μ–slip tyre model implemented in Simulink for pre-hardware validation
  • Custom PCB designed in EasyEDA and fabricated via JLCPCB — integrating LDO regulation, Hall sensor supply, and SD card telemetry logging
  • IMU-based ground speed estimation with deadzone compensation for slip ratio calculation
  • Telemetry analysis across wood and lab floor surfaces using Python/Matplotlib
Stack
ESP32 · C/C++ · FreeRTOS · MATLAB · Simulink · Proteus · EasyEDA · Python
University Team Project Hardware NeoPixel ★ Aston Open Days
02

2-Player NeoPixel Snake

Team Project · Electronic Procurement Lead

A two-player Snake game built on a custom-fabricated NeoPixel LED display, driven by an Arduino Mega. I led all electronic procurement — researching, testing, and selecting every component. The project was selected to be featured at Aston University Open Days as a showcase of student engineering work.

  • Custom NeoPixel display: two 16×8 matrices connected in series, forming a 16×16 addressable LED grid
  • Arduino Mega selected and validated as the controller — sufficient I/O and memory for two-player game logic
  • Component testing and evaluation prior to integration — reducing rework risk for the team
  • Power budget analysis to ensure stable 5 V supply across full LED load
  • BOM ownership: component selection, pricing, lead times, and supplier sourcing
  • Exhibited at Aston University Open Days — demonstrated live to prospective students
Stack / Role
Arduino Mega · NeoPixel · Electronic Procurement · BOM · Power Analysis
University Analogue Individual
03

Audio Amplifier

Individual Project · Aston University

A full end-to-end audio amplifier project taken from hand calculations through to simulation and physical manufacture — covering the complete engineering design process in a single deliverable.

  • Amplifier circuit designed from first principles — gain, biasing, and frequency response calculated by hand
  • Simulated in Proteus to validate performance before committing to hardware
  • PCB manufactured and physically assembled and tested
  • Full design process documented: calculation → simulation → build → test
Tools
Proteus · Analogue Circuit Design · PCB Manufacture
College Project Hardware Fusion 360
04

Pencil Case Speaker

Solo Project · Aston University Engineering Academy · Product Design & Electronics

Designed and manufactured a dual-purpose enclosure functioning as both a pencil case and a working speaker. The housing was modelled in Fusion 360 and physically fabricated, with a Kitronics PCB and speaker integrated into the final assembly.

  • Enclosure designed in Fusion 360 — dimensioned to house both stationery and electronics
  • Kitronics amplifier PCB and speaker selected and integrated into the chassis
  • Physical fabrication of the enclosure from design through to finished product
  • Practical constraint balancing: acoustic performance vs. form factor and usability
Tools
Fusion 360 · Kitronics PCB · Physical Fabrication · Product Design
Personal Arduino
05

Arduino Temperature Sensor

Personal Project · Independent Build

A self-initiated project built outside of university — designing and assembling a working temperature sensor using an Arduino. Demonstrates independent motivation to explore embedded hardware beyond coursework.

  • Sensor wiring, calibration, and serial output implemented independently
  • Project taken from concept to working prototype without academic guidance
  • Foundation for broader interest in embedded sensing and IoT systems
Tools
Arduino · C · Embedded Sensing
